title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Browser and Node Client Systems · Type Recall
summary: A design-system maintainer reconstructing an offline failure handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for https, tls identity, certificate trust, proxies, and downgrade boundaries, producing transport trust and certificate evidence record. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch transport trust and certificate evidence record after a delayed changed case.…
objectives: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Preserve hostname, chain, validity, algorithm, and revocation-policy checks without disabling verification. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Add an intended private trust root or test certificate without replacing or weakening unrelated trust. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Downgrade Mixed Content
steps.instruction:
review case — offline design-system client connects to api2.internal.test through proxy.example.test using a private CA and optional client certificate. Verify requested identity, connected endpoint, roots, proxy authority, downgrade policy, and what evidence the browser lab cannot supply. Competency probe: Reject unintended HTTP downgrades, insecure redirects, mixed content, and credential forwarding across weaker channels. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Inspect the Downgrade Mixed Content trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Fetch and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Downgrade Mixed Content.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for reject unintended http downgrades, insecure redirects, mixed content, and credential forwarding across weaker channels. demonstrate the result through typescript 7 strict types plus whatwg fetch request, response, headers, url, streams, and abortsignal contracts. For Fetch and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Downgrade Mixed Content, keep Downgrade Mixed Content connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
